# Jarvis Registry

> Jarvis Registry is an open-source, enterprise-grade MCP (Model Context Protocol) and A2A Agent Gateway built by ASCENDING Inc. It connects any AI copilot or autonomous agent to enterprise tools through a single secure gateway with identity-first access control, semantic tool discovery, and full observability.

Jarvis Registry solves the hardest problem in enterprise AI adoption: giving AI copilots and autonomous agents governed access to internal tools and data — without fragmented integrations, scattered credentials, or security blind spots.

It sits between your AI clients (Cursor, Claude Desktop, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, AWS Kiro) and your enterprise MCP servers and A2A agents. Every request is authenticated against your Identity Provider (Keycloak, Amazon Cognito, or Microsoft Entra ID) and checked against fine-grained ACL policies before a single tool is invoked.

**Publisher:** ASCENDING Inc — https://ascendingdc.com/jarvis-ai/
**GitHub:** https://github.com/ascending-llc/jarvis-registry
**Docs:** https://jarvisregistry.com/
**Explore Agentic** (field guide to enterprise agentic AI by ASCENDING): https://exploreagentic.ai/

**Tech stack:** Python 3.12, FastAPI, MongoDB (Beanie ODM), Weaviate vector DB, Redis, React 18 (Vite + TailwindCSS). All Python workspaces managed via `uv`.

**Key capabilities:**
- Universal MCP gateway — one authenticated endpoint for all AI copilots
- A2A Agent Registry — register, govern, and orchestrate autonomous agents
- Identity federation — Keycloak, Amazon Cognito, Microsoft Entra ID (OAuth 2.0/OIDC)
- Four-layer security — JWT auth → RBAC scope check → resource ACL → business logic
- Semantic tool discovery — Weaviate vector search + FlashRank reranker + hybrid tag matching
- External registry federation — Anthropic MCP Registry, Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, FoundryAI
- OpenTelemetry tracing, Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards

## Getting Started

- [Quick Start](https://jarvisregistry.com/quick-start/): Get Jarvis Registry running in 5 minutes with Docker Compose
- [Generate Secrets](https://jarvisregistry.com/generate-secrets/): Create all required credentials and secrets before first run
- [Production Deployment](https://jarvisregistry.com/deployment-guide/): Deploy to AWS, Azure, GCP, or Kubernetes
- [Configuration Reference](https://jarvisregistry.com/configuration/): All environment variables and settings

## Core Features

- [IDP Integration](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/auth-server/): Built-in OAuth 2.0/OIDC auth server supporting Keycloak, Cognito, and Entra ID
- [MCP Server Registry](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/mcp-registry/): Register, catalog, and proxy enterprise MCP servers with access control
- [A2A Agent Registry](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/a2a-registry/): Register and govern autonomous A2A agents alongside MCP servers
- [Registry Endpoint](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/registry-endpoint/): The single MCP entry point for AI copilots — one URL, all tools
- [AgentCore Federation](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/agentcore-federation/): Import and govern resources from Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
- [FoundryAI Federation](https://jarvisregistry.com/features/foundry-ai-federation/): Federate with FoundryAI agent registries

## Architecture & Design

- [Security Control Design](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/security-design/): Four-layer security model — authentication, RBAC, ACL, business logic
- [RBAC & Scopes](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/scopes/): Scope catalog, role definitions, and `scopes.yml` configuration
- [ACL Connector Service](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/acl-connector-service/): Resource-level access control for MCP servers and A2A agents
- [A2A Agent API Design](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/a2a-agent-api-design/): REST API design for autonomous agent registration and lifecycle
- [Agent Workflow](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/agent-workflow/): Orchestrator–worker agent workflow platform technical design
- [Federation Guide](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/federation/): Federate with Anthropic MCP Registry and Workday ASOR
- [DB Persistence](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/mcp_db_persistence_design/): MongoDB schema and Beanie document model design

## Why Jarvis Registry

- [Features Overview](https://jarvisregistry.com/FEATURES/): Full capability comparison and use case guide
- [Home / What It Does](https://jarvisregistry.com/): Architecture diagram and capability table

## Optional

- [Embeddings Configuration](https://jarvisregistry.com/embeddings/): Configure `embedded` (local FAISS) or `external` (Weaviate + Bedrock/OpenAI) mode
- [Keycloak Integration](https://jarvisregistry.com/keycloak-integration/): Step-by-step Keycloak setup with realm, clients, and group mappers
- [Entra ID Setup](https://jarvisregistry.com/entra-id-setup/): Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) configuration guide
- [AgentCore Integration Design](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/agentcore/): Deep-dive into Amazon Bedrock AgentCore integration architecture
- [Federation API](https://jarvisregistry.com/design/federation-api/): REST API reference for federation management endpoints
- [Contributing](https://jarvisregistry.com/CONTRIBUTING/): How to contribute — code, docs, and new IdP provider implementations
- [Security Policy](https://jarvisregistry.com/SECURITY/): Responsible disclosure and security reporting
